title: Payhip Alternatives for Restricted Countries: Which Option Actually Works for You
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: PayPal and Stripe block creators in dozens of countries. Here is the infrastructure that works everywhere they don't.

content.text: If you're a digital creator in Nigeria, Pakistan, or Ghana, you know the pain of PayPal, Stripe, Gumroad, or Payhip blocking your country. These platforms exclude entire regions due to banking regulations, political risks, or compliance costs. For creators selling ebooks, courses, or art, this means lost income and frustrated customers. Crypto-native infrastructure offers a permanent alternative, but not all solutions are equal. This comparison cuts through the noise to show which options actually work for restricted countries—and why.
What Each Option Actually Offers

PayPal, Stripe, Gumroad, and Payhip offer seamless payment processing, but only if you’re in an approved country. For restricted creators, these platforms are useless. Crypto-native solutions like Unchained Commerce’s Core Shop Engine ($49.99) provide backend infrastructure without requiring a payment processor account. Web3 Wallet Connect ($19.99) adds frontend wallet integration for 300+ coins. Payhip’s simplicity is its strength, but it fails outside its supported regions. Crypto wins by default—no KYC, no geographic blocks, and lifetime access.
How They Handle Restricted Countries

PayPal and Stripe exclude countries like Nigeria, Iran, and Venezuela due to regulatory risks. Gumroad and Payhip rely on these processors, so they inherit the same restrictions. Crypto-native infrastructure bypasses this entirely. Unchained Commerce’s Full Stack Bundle ($79.99) includes everything needed to sell globally without touching a blocked payment processor. Payhip’s country support list is clear but limited. Crypto is the only option that structurally solves the exclusion problem.
Payment Speed, Fees, and Real Costs

PayPal and Stripe charge 2.9% + $0.30 per transaction, but only if you’re allowed to use them. Payhip adds its own 5% fee on top. Crypto transactions via NOWPayments cost 0.5% with no buyer account required. Unchained Commerce’s one-time payment model means no recurring fees. Payhip’s fees are higher, and its reliance on PayPal/Stripe means additional delays. Crypto delivers faster payouts and lower costs for both you and your buyers.
PayPal and Stripe exclude entire countries, making them useless for most global creators.
Automation and Delivery Capabilities

Payhip automates delivery but requires PayPal or Stripe, which fails in restricted countries. Unchained Commerce’s Core Shop Engine automates delivery, payment verification, and PDF receipts without needing a payment processor account. The Admin Dashboard ($29.99) provides transaction logs and real-time analytics, giving you full control. Payhip’s automation is useless if you can’t use its payment gateways. Crypto-native infrastructure ensures your store runs smoothly, no matter where you’re based.
Which One to Choose Based on Your Situation

If you’re in a restricted country, PayPal, Stripe, Gumroad, and Payhip are non-starters. Crypto-native solutions are your only reliable option. Unchained Commerce’s Full Stack Bundle ($79.99) is the best choice for creators needing global reach, no KYC, and lifetime access. If you’re a solo developer building a crypto-native store, start with Web3 Wallet Connect ($19.99). Payhip is only viable if you’re in a supported country. For everyone else, crypto wins—no competition.
How do I sell digital products if PayPal is not available in my country?

If PayPal is unavailable in your country, crypto-native infrastructure is the best alternative. Platforms like Unchained Commerce allow you to sell digital products globally without relying on blocked payment processors. By accepting 300+ cryptocurrencies, you bypass geographic restrictions and banking limitations. This solution is fully automated, requires no KYC, and provides instant delivery—making it ideal for creators in restricted regions.
Crypto-native infrastructure provides a permanent solution to geographic payment blocks.
